mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-increasing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending corporate revenue contraction and recession risk, causing equity markets to price in lower earnings and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the threshold, or t-statistic of return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate profit growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects tightening capacity utilization and rising corporate output, which translates into immediate positive earnings revisions and short-term equity price appreciation.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, or out-of-sample hit rate drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the 310 index level forces monetary authorities to maintain restrictive policy, increasing real discount rates and triggering negative equity returns as growth expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 310, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll growth and unemployment decline confirms a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive 60-day returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal rising construction activity and durable goods demand, which boosts near-term corporate earnings expectations and generates positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.01 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, prompting equity market sell-offs and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances following two consecutive INDPRO declines, invalidating the contraction signal.
